scope.summary: CF3 migrates legacy audit state through verified capability directory handles, no-follow operations, identity checks, and an atomic create-new hard-link install that refuses destination replacement.
out_of_scope: The migration's layout policy and legacy/current conflict handling are unchanged.
No general filesystem sandbox or unrelated doctor repair behavior is claimed.

The supporting cap_std and cap_fs_ext dependency entries landed with ee1b23ad, but the generated Cargo.lock is deliberately excluded from this source-behavior close scope because another pane currently owns a separate lockfile hunk.

entry_points.name: apply_legacy_state_migration
entry_points.trace: Implementing commit ee1b23ad reads and creates via cap_std directory handles traversed with open_dir_nofollow, writes 0600 create-new files and fsyncs both file and directory, validates parent and file device/inode identities, and atomically hard-links the verified temp to the final name. A hard link preserves create-new semantics where a pathname rename would overwrite a destination introduced during the race. legacy_migration_refuses_symlink_swaps_at_atomic_install deterministically replaces the parent and final destination with symlinks; the code refuses before writing through either replacement. The existing normal migration test retains byte-identical current/backup files and the legacy source.

entry_points.name: scoped compile verification
entry_points.trace: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/home/durakovic/projects/oraclemcp/target-lavendertern cargo check -p oraclemcp-core --tests --locked completed successfully after the implementation, compiling the migration and deterministic race-hook tests. cargo fmt --all -- --check also completed successfully.

limitations: Scoped cargo check compiled the tests; the runtime test suite was not run because the operator limited this wave to scoped cargo check commands.
The deterministic symlink-race regression test is Unix-only, matching the filesystem behavior it asserts.
